story.text: The gates govern what crosses between the **git canon** (source of truth) and the **federated wiki**. They are read off the existing canon ‚Äî *Librarian forks / Archivist seals*, the coherence FAIL-blocks, the chronicle gates OPEN‚ÜíSIGNED, and the vendored-vs-mine attribution discipline. Every page crossing git ‚Üí wiki passes **G0‚ÄìG6**; every contribution crossing wiki ‚Üí git passes **G7**.

## Out ‚Äî projecting the canon (G0‚ÄìG6)

- **G0 ¬∑ Source of Truth** ‚Äî the git repo is always upstream. Nothing canonical is authored first on the wiki; a wiki page is a fork that records its origin. *Librarian forks; Archivist never copies.*
- **G1 ¬∑ Classification** ‚Äî every file is marked *circulating* (federate) or *sealed* (stay in git: chronicles, plans, drafts, version-history, audits). Only circulating content passes.
- **G2 ¬∑ Coherence** ‚Äî labels/slots/numbers match the canon: conjecture numbers vs the register, cast tiers/sigils vs the head grimoire, no foreign labels.
- **G3 ¬∑ Attribution** ‚Äî every page carries `upstream:`, author, and license; vendored content is credited to its real author, never to us.
- **G4 ¬∑ Projection** ‚Äî content is shown at the right compression altitude (spellbook‚Üínarrative‚Üískill‚Üíproverb‚Üíspell); the guide surfaces the transmissible layer and links *down* to source.
- **G5 ¬∑ Hyperlink coherence** ‚Äî cross-links derive from the graph edges; every link resolves; no orphans.
- **G6 ¬∑ Distribution** ‚Äî content routes to the correct sibling site under canonical slugs; no cross-site collisions.

## Back ‚Äî contributions into the canon (G7)

- **G7 ¬∑ Fork-back** ‚Äî an external edit returns to the git canon only through coherence (G2) + attribution (G3) + a **First-Person sign-off** (OPEN‚ÜíSIGNED). The page journal is the lineage; a merge-back step proposes a diff into the source repo for signing ‚Äî it never auto-commits.
